# Configuration Reference

> **Status**: Living document. Sections marked `<!-- AUTO-GENERATED -->`
> are regenerated by `/update-docs`; everything else is hand-written prose.
> **Last regenerated**: 2026-04-26 (late)

Shikigami uses **`~/.shikigami/config.json`** as its single user-config
surface (no `.env` file). Settings are loaded once at startup; some need
a restart, others apply on save (see the **Live update?** column).

---

## ~/.shikigami/ Layout

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:home-layout -->

```
~/.shikigami/
├── config.json        # User settings (see below)
├── token              # Bearer token for /v1/* endpoints (chmod 0600)
├── characters/        # Optional user-installed characters (overrides repo dir)
├── tts/               # Generated TTS audio — auto-purged after 1h
└── logs/              # (reserved) future tracing output
```

<!-- /AUTO-GENERATED:home-layout -->

The token file is created automatically on first launch. Hook scripts
read both `token` and `config.json` on every invocation, so rotating the
token does not require a Shikigami restart for the hook side.

---

## config.json Schema

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:settings -->

Mirrors `src-tauri/src/config/settings.rs::Settings`.

| Field                      | Type    | Default     | Live update? | Purpose                                                           |
|----------------------------|---------|-------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `port`                     | u16     | `7796`      | No (restart) | Preferred event-server port; falls forward up to +10 on conflict  |
| `active_character`         | string? | `null`      | No           | Character `id` from `characters/<id>/manifest.json`               |
| `click_through`            | bool    | `false`     | **Yes**      | Smart click-through — only the character region catches clicks; transparent surroundings pass clicks through to apps below. Polling-based (60Hz) hit-test in `passthrough.rs`. |
| `opacity`                  | f32     | `1.0`       | Yes          | 0.2 – 1.0 — overall character alpha. Applied via `app.stage.alpha` after `settings_changed` Tauri event. |
| `scale`                    | f32     | `1.0`       | Yes          | 0.3 – 2.0 — extra user multiplier on top of auto-fit (90% containment). Hard-capped so character never overflows window. |
| `auto_hide_during_capture` | bool    | `true`      | (planned)    | Hide window when screen-capture is detected                        |
| `tts.provider`             | string  | `"none"`    | No (restart) | `none` / `say-macos` / `piper` / `openai` / `elevenlabs`           |
| `tts.voice`                | string? | `null`      | No (next call) | Provider-specific voice id (see TTS Providers below)             |
| `tts.api_key`              | string? | `null`      | No (next call) | Cloud provider key (env var wins — see below)                    |
| `tts.piper_binary`         | string? | `null`      | No           | Path to `piper` binary; defaults to `$PATH` lookup                 |
| `tts.piper_model`          | string? | `null`      | No           | Path to a `.onnx` Piper voice model                                |
| `tts.rate`                 | f32     | `1.0`       | No (next call) | Speech rate multiplier (0.5 – 2.0)                                |
| `tts.volume`               | f32     | `1.0`       | Yes (next playback) | 0.0 – 1.0 — playback volume; 0 = mute (skips synth + lipsync) |

### Example

```json
{
  "port": 7796,
  "active_character": "hiyori",
  "click_through": false,
  "opacity": 1.0,
  "scale": 1.0,
  "auto_hide_during_capture": true,
  "tts": {
    "provider": "say-macos",
    "voice": "Linh",
    "api_key": null,
    "piper_binary": null,
    "piper_model": null,
    "rate": 1.0,
    "volume": 1.0
  }
}
```

<!-- /AUTO-GENERATED:settings -->

---

## Environment Variables

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:env -->

There is no `.env` file. The few env vars Shikigami honours are:

| Variable              | Consumer            | Required | Effect                                                       |
|-----------------------|---------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `SHIKIGAMI_LOG`       | Rust backend         | No       | `tracing-subscriber` filter (e.g. `debug`, `shikigami=trace`) |
| `SHIKIGAMI_HOME`      | Hook bridge         | No       | Override `~/.shikigami` location                              |
| `SHIKIGAMI_URL`       | Hook bridge         | No       | Full event-server URL — bypasses port lookup                 |
| `SHIKIGAMI_DEBUG`     | Hook bridge         | No       | `=1` → write transform / POST errors to stderr                |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY`      | TTS / OpenAI        | Cond.    | Required when `tts.provider = "openai"` (env wins over JSON) |
| `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`  | TTS / ElevenLabs    | Cond.    | Required when `tts.provider = "elevenlabs"`                  |
| `TAURI_DEV_HOST`      | Vite (build only)   | No       | Bind dev server to a non-loopback host (mobile testing)      |

<!-- /AUTO-GENERATED:env -->

---

## TTS Providers

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:tts -->

| Provider     | Latency | Cost     | Offline | Voice format                                  | Output |
|--------------|---------|----------|---------|-----------------------------------------------|--------|
| `none`       | —       | —        | —       | (disabled)                                    | —      |
| `say-macos`  | ~300ms  | Free     | ✅      | macOS voice name (`Linh`, `Samantha`, …)      | AIFF   |
| `piper`      | ~200ms  | Free     | ✅      | (set via `piper_model` `.onnx` path)          | WAV    |
| `openai`     | ~500ms  | $0.015/1k chars | ❌ | `alloy`, `nova`, `shimmer`, `echo`, …       | MP3    |
| `elevenlabs` | ~700ms  | $$       | ❌      | voice id (e.g. `21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM`)        | MP3    |

Discover macOS voices: `say -v "?"`. Discover ElevenLabs voices via
`https://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/voices`.

<!-- /AUTO-GENERATED:tts -->

---

## Event API

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:api -->

Bind: `127.0.0.1:<port>` (default 7796). Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

### `POST /v1/events`

Body shape mirrors `src-tauri/src/event/schema.rs::EventPayload`:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": "1.0",
  "source": "claude-code",
  "type": "tool_complete",
  "tool": "Bash",
  "exitCode": 0,
  "durationMs": 1234,
  "severity": "info",
  "text": "OK",
  "metadata": { "anything": true },
  "sessionId": "claude-code-tab-uuid",
  "cwd": "/Users/.../my-repo"
}
```

`sessionId` + `cwd` are optional. When present, the backend's
`SessionRegistry` records the session; events from sessions the user
muted via Preferences are dropped silently (still returns `202`).

Allowed `type`: `session_start`, `session_end`, `session_idle_short`,
`session_idle_long`, `user_prompt`, `assistant_message`, `tool_start`,
`tool_complete`, `error`, `destructive_op_detected`, `git_commit`,
`git_push`.

Allowed `severity`: `info`, `warning`, `error`, `critical`.

Returns `202 Accepted` on success, `4xx`/`5xx` with JSON `{ error }` on failure.

### `POST /v1/say`

Synthesise text to speech via the configured provider.

```json
{ "text": "Xin chào chủ nhân", "voice": "Linh" }
```

`voice` is optional — overrides `tts.voice` for this call only. Returns
the absolute audio path:

```json
{ "audio_url": "/Users/.../tts/<uuid>.aiff", "mime": "audio/aiff", "provider": "say-macos" }
```

Backend also emits the Tauri event `tts:speak` so the renderer can drive
Live2D mouth-sync via `model.speak(audioUrl)`.

`503 Service Unavailable` is returned when `tts.provider = "none"`.

### Tauri commands (frontend ↔ backend, not HTTP)

Invoked from the renderer via `@tauri-apps/api/core::invoke()`:

| Command                    | Purpose                                                       |
|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| `ping`                     | Health check; returns `"pong"`                                |
| `get_settings`             | Read persisted `Settings` from `~/.shikigami/config.json`     |
| `update_settings`          | Merge a partial patch + persist + emit `settings_changed`     |
| `apply_runtime_settings_cmd` | Re-apply window-level settings (click-through) without restart |
| `list_characters`          | Enumerate loaded characters with metadata                     |
| `get_active_character`     | Full payload of the currently-active character                |
| `set_active_character`     | Switch active character by id (frontend then full-reloads)    |
| `list_sessions`            | List Claude Code sessions seen since startup                  |
| `set_session_allowed`      | Mute / unmute events from a specific session id               |
| `set_character_bbox`       | Push the rendered character's screen-space AABB to the smart-passthrough hit-tester |

### Tauri events (backend → frontend)

| Event              | Payload                | When                                               |
|--------------------|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| `state_changed`    | `ResolvedState`        | After every accepted event in `/v1/events`         |
| `tts:speak`        | `SpeakEvent`           | After every successful `/v1/say` synthesis (or idle nudge) |
| `settings_changed` | `Settings`             | After every `update_settings` invocation           |
| `tray:toggle_diag` | `()`                   | User clicked tray "Toggle Diagnostics"             |
| `tray:open_settings` | `()`                 | User clicked tray "Preferences…"                   |
| `tray:position_reset` | `()`                | User clicked tray "Reset Position"                 |

<!-- /AUTO-GENERATED:api -->

---

## Character Packages

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:character -->

A character is a directory under `characters/<id>/` containing a
`manifest.json` and asset tree. Schema mirrors
`src-tauri/src/character/manifest.rs::CharacterManifest`.

### Minimum manifest

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://shikigami.dev/schema/manifest/v1.0.json",
  "schemaVersion": "1.0",
  "id": "my-char",
  "name": "My Character",
  "author": "you",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "license": "CC-BY-4.0",
  "renderer": "live2d",
  "defaultState": "idle",
  "states": {
    "idle":    { "path": "assets/states/idle",    "fps": 30, "loop": true,  "motion": "Idle" },
    "happy":   { "path": "assets/states/happy",   "fps": 30, "loop": false, "then": "idle", "durationMs": 2000, "motion": "TapBody" },
    "focused": { "path": "assets/states/focused", "fps": 30, "loop": true,  "motion": "Idle" },
    "warning": { "path": "assets/states/warning", "fps": 30, "loop": true,  "motion": "TapBody" },
    "sleepy":  { "path": "assets/states/sleepy",  "fps": 30, "loop": true,  "motion": "Idle" }
  }
}
```

Required states: **`idle`** and **`happy`** (validated by
`CharacterManifest::validate`). Allowed `renderer`: `sprite` or `live2d`.

### Live2D layout

```
characters/<id>/
├── manifest.json
├── LICENSE
└── assets/states/
    ├── idle/
    │   ├── frame_00.model3.json   (symlink to <Model>.model3.json)
    │   ├── <Model>.moc3
    │   ├── <Model>.<size>/texture_*.png
    │   └── motions/*.motion3.json
    ├── happy   → idle    (symlink)
    ├── focused → idle
    ├── warning → idle
    └── sleepy  → idle
```

`motion` field maps a Shikigami state to a Cubism motion-group name
(`"Idle"` / `"TapBody"` / etc.). Inspect a model's groups:

```bash
cat <model>.model3.json | python3 -c \
  "import json,sys; print(list((json.load(sys.stdin)['FileReferences'].get('Motions') or {}).keys()))"
```

### Sprite layout

```
characters/<id>/assets/states/<name>/frame_NN.{png,webp}
```

Frames sorted alphabetically by filename, played at the manifest's `fps`.

<!-- /AUTO-GENERATED:character -->

---

## Claude Code Hook Wiring

Use `python3 scripts/install-hook.py install` (idempotent) to add five
entries under `hooks` in `~/.claude/settings.json`:

| Claude Code event   | Bridge → Shikigami                                                  |
|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `PreToolUse`        | `tool_start` (or `destructive_op_detected` for matching `Bash` cmds) — tool name drives the per-tool dominant in `state/machine.rs` (e.g. `Task`/`WebFetch` → Confused, `TodoWrite` → Happy) |
| `PostToolUse`       | `tool_complete` with exit code (success → Happy, except `Read`/`Grep`/`Glob` → quiet Idle) |
| `UserPromptSubmit`  | `user_prompt`                                                         |
| `Stop`              | `session_end` **+** `/v1/say` of the last assistant message (markdown-stripped, capped 400 chars) |
| `SessionStart`      | `session_start`                                                       |

Each event also forwards Claude Code's `session_id` + `cwd` so the
backend session picker can attribute / mute per-tab.

Verify wiring + token + script perms: `python3 scripts/install-hook.py doctor`.

## Idle Behaviour

After 10 minutes with no real events the backend (`state/idle_timer.rs`)
synthesises a `SessionIdleLong` → resolves to `Sleepy` state. If TTS is
configured, also speaks one of `IDLE_NUDGE_MESSAGES` (currently 5
Vietnamese nudges — "Anh ơi đang làm gì đó?", etc.). Reset on the next
real event.
